text:
Mei Lin: seeing a mismatch in the reconciliation view vs billing totals for last week. dashboard shows ~6.3% lower $ than billing for hosted/self-serve in us-east. anyone else looking at this?

Cole Summers: is this from the new reconciliation report view (ENG-8430) or ad-hoc?

Mei Lin: from `finance.unit_econ_recon_daily_v1` (the one from pr-19390). comparing to billing export totals by day.

Rishi Malhotra: yup, i flagged the same in the KPI pack draft. for Jan 25, billing says $128,442 (hosted), dashboard alloc says $120,081. tokens are closer though.

Laura Bennett: we need to understand if it’s a definition gap vs a bug. reminder: finance signoff assumes we can reconcile within ~1-2% at monthly granularity.

Logan Wright: can you drop the exact filters / query? this smells like (a) sampling window mismatch (UTC vs PT day boundary) or (b) tier mapping excluding some routes.

Mei L...

title: #eng-platform: 1738095544
preview:
Kevin Osei: Quick q on `schema_version` strategy for audit events + exporter normalization. For v1 rollout: do we require every producer to set `schema_version`, or can exporter backfill it when it recognizes a legacy shape?

Kevin Osei: Context: exporter is currently seeing a mix of shapes in the sink (some older c...
text:
Kevin Osei: Quick q on `schema_version` strategy for audit events + exporter normalization. For v1 rollout: do we require every producer to set `schema_version`, or can exporter backfill it when it recognizes a legacy shape?

Kevin Osei: Context: exporter is currently seeing a mix of shapes in the sink (some older control plane stuff still has `tenantId`, `action`, etc). Need something deterministic for SIEM customers.

Michael Nguyen: My take: producers MUST emit `schema_version` once they adopt v1. But exporter should also *normalize* legacy events and set `schema_version` on the *normalized* output.

Michael Nguyen: Otherwise customers get a split-brain stream forever.

Nadia Rahman: +1. Telemetry side also needs to segment by version. If `schema_version` is missing, dashboards become “maybe v1, maybe not” :grimacing:

Allison Grant: we should be explicit in the schema doc: `schema...

title: #eng-platform: 1738013377
preview:
Ethan Park: We need a control-plane endpoint that reports backup readiness/status for Private. Question: do we stick it into existing control-plane API service, or spin up a new “backup subsystem” service?

Vivek Kulkarni: +1. Customers are asking “prove to me backups are running” and “what’s last good backup” witho...
text:
Ethan Park: We need a control-plane endpoint that reports backup readiness/status for Private. Question: do we stick it into existing control-plane API service, or spin up a new “backup subsystem” service?

Vivek Kulkarni: +1. Customers are asking “prove to me backups are running” and “what’s last good backup” without SSH’ing into k8s. Console also needs something consistent.

Benji Okafor: Current installer `backup create` prints status locally, but for scheduled CronJob we don’t have a nice remote view other than scraping logs/metrics.

Allison Grant: From obs side, we’ll have metrics + dashboards, but UI/automation wants an API response with last_success_ts + last_error + duration. Metrics alone won’t satisfy audits.

Priya Natarajan: Preference: minimal surface area, no new service unless necessary. What’s the cleanest place in the control plane today?

Ethan Park: Options I see:
...

title: #eng-platform: 1737924411
preview: Logan Wright: heads up: I think we have a gap for Console results dashboard after the attribution schema update. We added `{experiment_id, variant_id}` dims to the rollups, but existing rollup partitions (and some downstream cached datasets) won’t have those columns populated -> Console is going to show “no data” / ...
text:
Logan Wright: heads up: I think we have a gap for Console results dashboard after the attribution schema update. We added `{experiment_id, variant_id}` dims to the rollups, but existing rollup partitions (and some downstream cached datasets) won’t have those columns populated -> Console is going to show “no data” / flatlines for anything before deploy.

Mei Lin: yep, confirmed. The `route_variant_metrics_rollup` table is partitioned by hour and the new columns are NULL for pre-change partitions. The results API query groups by variant, so it drops the old partitions on the floor.

Chloe Martin: that explains what I’m seeing in staging — list/detail loads but charts only start at ~this morning. I assumed it was just low traffic :facepalm:

Caleb Johnson: +1. Ingestion is fine; it’s the aggregation layer. Raw spans have the attrs for ~N days, but the rollup job only started writing the ...

title: #eng-platform: 1737750121
preview:
Logan Wright: Hey folks — need quick alignment on OTel span attribute names for route experiments before we lock PR-28489. We already tag `rw.route.id`, `rw.model.id`, etc. What should we use for experiment + variant?

Caleb Johnson: +1. Current draft in the PR uses `experiment_id` / `variant_id` (flat) which doesn’...
text:
Logan Wright: Hey folks — need quick alignment on OTel span attribute names for route experiments before we lock PR-28489. We already tag `rw.route.id`, `rw.model.id`, etc. What should we use for experiment + variant?

Caleb Johnson: +1. Current draft in the PR uses `experiment_id` / `variant_id` (flat) which doesn’t match anything else we do. Also worried about collisions with generic “experiment” usage.

Allison Grant: We should follow our existing convention: lowercase, vendor prefix, dot-separated. OTel spec doesn’t require dots, but it’s what we’ve been doing for custom attrs (`rw.*`).

Jordan Lee: On the routing side we have fields named `experiment_id` and `variant_id` in the request context. Happy to map to whatever attribute keys we standardize.

Jada Williams: Also: please don’t add both “route_experiment” and “canary” attributes with different naming styles. Console is goin...

title: #eng-platform: 1737381192
preview:
Logan Wright: Kicking off thread on the usage-insights aggregated dataset design. We need to lock: time bucket granularity, partitioning, late events handling, and who owns any backfill. Goal is route+model breakdowns that reconcile w/ billing.

Nadia Rahman: +1. Billing side constraint: invoices are computed in dai...
text:
Logan Wright: Kicking off thread on the usage-insights aggregated dataset design. We need to lock: time bucket granularity, partitioning, late events handling, and who owns any backfill. Goal is route+model breakdowns that reconcile w/ billing.

Nadia Rahman: +1. Billing side constraint: invoices are computed in daily windows (UTC) but customers view in local time in console. We can *display* local-time but the dataset should be anchored in UTC or we’ll never reconcile.

Chloe Martin: For console queries we need interactive latency. If we bucket hourly we get nicer “what changed” and charts, but query cost might be higher. If we bucket daily it’s easier but UX wants hourly drilldowns.

Thomas Baird: On partitioning: please don’t do hour partitions at the top-level unless we’re 100% sure. Warehouse (Snowflake) likes partitions by day and clustering on dimensions. Hour partitions explod...

title: #eng-platform: 1737332112
preview: Nadia Rahman: Kicking off the metric definition alignment thread for the Unit Econ dashboard. We need a crisp definition for “blended $/1M output tokens” that Finance will sign off on and Eng can implement consistently. Biggest open items: (1) idle capacity, (2) shared overhead (control plane / observability / suppo...
text:
Nadia Rahman: Kicking off the metric definition alignment thread for the Unit Econ dashboard. We need a crisp definition for “blended $/1M output tokens” that Finance will sign off on and Eng can implement consistently. Biggest open items: (1) idle capacity, (2) shared overhead (control plane / observability / support), (3) dedicated/private pools.

Logan Wright: +1. We keep saying “blended” but people interpret it as marginal. We should be explicit: numerator is what cost buckets, denominator is what tokens (output only vs total), and what time window / allocation basis.

Rishi Malhotra: From FP&A perspective, blended should reconcile to infra spend. That means idle is *in* (otherwise you’ll never tie out). The question is *where* we allocate it and whether we show a separate “active-only” view.

Cole Summers: Current draft in the PRD says primary metric = total cost / total output t...

title: #eng-platform: 1736964123
preview:
Ethan Park: kicking off a thread on initial design details for optimization config versioning. Need alignment on (1) what is a “version”, (2) scoping (tenant/model/region), (3) migration from existing configs.

Benji Okafor: +1. Starting point I’ve been thinking: `OptimizationConfigVersion` is immutable blob + metad...
text:
Ethan Park: kicking off a thread on initial design details for optimization config versioning. Need alignment on (1) what is a “version”, (2) scoping (tenant/model/region), (3) migration from existing configs.

Benji Okafor: +1. Starting point I’ve been thinking: `OptimizationConfigVersion` is immutable blob + metadata. Separate “active” pointer per scope.

Jordan Lee: when we say version: is it just a monotonically increasing integer per config id, or a global uuid? I’d prefer opaque ID + `created_at`, `created_by`, `parent_version_id` (optional) for diffs.

Michael Nguyen: storage-wise easiest: `config_versions` table with `version_id (uuid)` PK, `config_id` FK, `seq` int unique per config_id. seq is nice for humans and for rollback selection.

Ethan Park: ok so “version” = immutable snapshot. question: do we have 1 config per tenant or per (tenant, model, region)??

Benji Okafor: I...

title: #eng-platform: 1736879041
preview:
Jada Williams: quick review request: request-context plumbing for route experiments → tracing/metrics. want to sanity check we’re propagating `{experiment_id, variant_id, config_version}` consistently across router -> runtime -> otel spans -> metrics emitters. code pointers:
- `redwood/router/request_context.go` (ne...
text:
Jada Williams: quick review request: request-context plumbing for route experiments → tracing/metrics. want to sanity check we’re propagating `{experiment_id, variant_id, config_version}` consistently across router -> runtime -> otel spans -> metrics emitters. code pointers:
- `redwood/router/request_context.go` (new fields on `RequestContext`)
- `redwood/policy/experiment_evaluator.go` (assignment)
- `redwood/observability/otel/span_tags.go` (tagging)
PRs: `pr-28470` + `pr-28489`. anything we’re missing before we cut beta?

Jordan Lee: looked this morning. overall shape good. 2 things:
1) please make sure we set assignment *before* fallback policy eval (otherwise we’ll sometimes tag spans with the fallback model’s “effective” selection but no experiment fields). i think `pr-28457` changed order but i want to confirm `RequestContext` gets populated in the same block.
2) determinism: a...

title: #eng-platform: 1736623349
preview:
Jordan Lee: Quick Q we need to settle for route experiments: what’s the precedence if a route is in an active canary rollout *and* an experiment is configured on the same route? (we already have a few internal routes that might do this by accident)

Mina Haddad: +1, we’ll get this from customers too. Canary today is...
text:
Jordan Lee: Quick Q we need to settle for route experiments: what’s the precedence if a route is in an active canary rollout *and* an experiment is configured on the same route? (we already have a few internal routes that might do this by accident)

Mina Haddad: +1, we’ll get this from customers too. Canary today is effectively “traffic split stable vs canary target”, and experiment is “traffic split variants”. If we let both run we’re basically double-bucketing.

Ravi Menon: Yeah double hashing = hard to reason about + noisy metrics. Also attribution becomes messy: are we comparing experiment variant deltas or rollout deltas? It’s like 2 independent variables.

Marissa Cole: From Console POV I’d really like a simple story. Either we block it or we have a deterministic rule and we surface a big warning.

Dylan Brooks: UX-wise we can probably block creation when there’s an active rollo...

title: #eng-platform: 1736538810
preview:
Jordan Lee: seeing weird allocation skew on route experiments when stickiness key is missing. In staging + 1 beta acct, variant B basically gets ~0-2% even though split is 50/50.

Jordan Lee: suspecting we're hashing `""` or a super low-entropy fallback in the evaluator. can someone from telemetry sanity check wheth...
text:
Jordan Lee: seeing weird allocation skew on route experiments when stickiness key is missing. In staging + 1 beta acct, variant B basically gets ~0-2% even though split is 50/50.

Jordan Lee: suspecting we're hashing `""` or a super low-entropy fallback in the evaluator. can someone from telemetry sanity check whether `stickiness_key_present=false` correlates?

Logan Wright: yep. quick query on staging rollup: when `sticky_key_source=missing` we see almost all requests land in the same bucket.

Logan Wright: pasted from my scratchpad:
```
variant distribution (missing key):
A: 98.7%
B: 1.3%

variant distribution (customer_id):
A: 50.4%
B: 49.6%
```

Ethan Park: :eyes: do we know what the evaluator uses today when missing? last I remember we had precedence order: `x-redwood-sticky-key` -> `end_user_id` -> `customer_id` -> else ???

Jordan Lee: current code path does:
- if none found, `...

title: #eng-platform: 1736039442
preview:
Ethan Park: seeing weird config propagation lag in staging for route experiments. console says experiment started but router still serving control for ~6-10 mins. anyone else noticing?

Benji Okafor: yeah, hit it twice today. `experiment-service` shows state=RUNNING + config_version incremented, but policy engine ev...
text:
Ethan Park: seeing weird config propagation lag in staging for route experiments. console says experiment started but router still serving control for ~6-10 mins. anyone else noticing?

Benji Okafor: yeah, hit it twice today. `experiment-service` shows state=RUNNING + config_version incremented, but policy engine evaluator still pulling old snapshot.

Jordan Lee: +1. I was debugging split skew and noticed the evaluator using `config_version=118` while control plane already at `124`.

Mina Haddad: is this only staging? prod seems ok (but lower churn).

Rafael Mendes: if lag is real we should get ahead of it before beta expansion. do we have a metric for “config staleness” at router/policy layer?

Michael Nguyen: we have `config_cache_hit` and `config_refresh_ms` but nothing directly comparing expected version vs observed version.

Ethan Park: dropping some logs. from experiment-service...

title: #eng-platform: 1723456789
preview:
Maya Patel: Quick question — our canary dashboard currently shows p50/p95/p99 comparisons vs baseline, but it's hard to tell significance for low-traffic routes. Can we add a 'stat sig' column and a drilldown that shows per-customer slices?
Jorge Ramos: +1, especially for routes that only see a few qps. Right now th...
text:
Maya Patel: Quick question — our canary dashboard currently shows p50/p95/p99 comparisons vs baseline, but it's hard to tell significance for low-traffic routes. Can we add a 'stat sig' column and a drilldown that shows per-customer slices?
Jorge Ramos: +1, especially for routes that only see a few qps. Right now the alert triggers spam on small samples.
Sasha Li: We could compute bootstrapped CIs and mark metrics as 'insufficient data' under N<100 requests.
Ben Carter: I like that. Also want a way to exclude canary traffic under quota throttling so the split math isn't skewed.
Maya Patel: Thinking UX: in the rollout page, show canary vs baseline, delta, CI, and a small sparkline. Clicking delta opens a side panel with breakdown by region, route, customer-tier.
Jorge Ramos: For alerts, we should support 'absolute' and 'relative' guards. Relative uses percent delta > x AND statisticall...

title: #eng-platform: 1719876543
preview:
Ravi Patel: FYI — I'm seeing a weird pattern where the routing sampler flips a coin for some requests and our decision logs are empty for those. Tenant: acme-prod.
Ava Chen: which sampler? the global one or the route-level override? :thinking_face:
Ravi Patel: looks like route-level. sampler config shows something l...
text:
Ravi Patel: FYI — I'm seeing a weird pattern where the routing sampler flips a coin for some requests and our decision logs are empty for those. Tenant: acme-prod.
Ava Chen: which sampler? the global one or the route-level override? :thinking_face:
Ravi Patel: looks like route-level. sampler config shows something like `type: coinflip; p: 0.01` but the traces don't show any eval spans.
Tom G: could be header precedence. We recently added `x-sampling-override: force` and that short-circuits decision logging unless the decision-log flag is explicit.
Ravi Patel: interesting — I captured a request where header `x-sampling`=\"1\" but decision log was absent. curl I used: `curl -H \"Authorization: Bearer XYZ\" -H \"x-sampling: 1\" https://api.redwood.test/v1/route`
Ava Chen: can you paste the route rule? maybe the rule matcher causes a fallback early and the sampler runs after the short-cir...

text:
Priya Rao: Hey — ran into a weird charge on staging: client reconnects after EOF while a toolcall was in-flight, and the ledger shows both the prefix and the truncated toolcall counted twice.
Ben Carter: logs?
Priya Rao: pasted minimal trace:
```json
{"session_id":"s-abc123","route":"r-1","event":"toolcall","tokens_charged":42}
{"session_id":"s-abc123","route":"r-2","event":"reconnect","tokens_charged":42}
```
Marco Li: so route switched during reconnect? that could re-emit the toolcall accounting
Li Zhang: this is the case we discussed where router handoff doesn't dedupe provisional credits
Priya Rao: repro steps: start streaming -> send toolcall -> induce EOF on network -> client reconnects to different route -> resume -> toolcall truncated => double charge
Ben Carter: is the double charge provisional vs confirmed or both confirmed?
Priya Rao: both confirmed in ledger, UI shows two ...
